channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Protesters march at Nakba Day rallies around Australia
item.description: Crowds gather at rallies around the country to commemorate the anniversary of the Nakba, a day that recognises the mass displacement of Palestinians in 1948.

media:group.media:description: Protesters gathered in Adelaide's city centre on Sunday to mark Nakba Day.

item.title: Hobart military barracks slated for housing development
item.description: Up to 1,000 new homes could be built on the site of a military barracks in Hobart, under a state-federal redevelopment agreement.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: The proposal will replace the Derwent Barracks, which is to be sold as part of a national defence estate sell-off.

item.title: Rousey stops Carano 17 seconds into their double comeback MMA bout
item.description: After an almost 10-year absence from MMA, Ronda Rousey makes light work of Gina Carano in their highly publicised fight in Los Angeles.

media:group.media:description: Ronda Rousey (left) and Gina Carano returned to the cage in los Angeles.

item.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim
item.description: The wife of a 38-year-old Perth man who died in a shark attack off the coast of Rottnest Island in Western Australia on Saturday describes him as a devoted father and avid spearfisherman.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Williams and Irena Ceranic

media:group.media:description: Steven Mattaboni, pictured with his wife Shirene and two young daughters, was killed in the shark attack.

item.title: Live: NSW confirms Latrell Mitchell will miss Origin opener with back injury
item.description: South Sydney and NSW star Latrell Mitchell will miss State of Origin I after aggravating a back injury during Magic Round. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Mitchell was ruled out of the Origin opener after aggravating his back injury during Magic Round.

item.title: How Pokémon cards are fuelling crime in Melbourne
item.description: Rare Pokémon cards can sell for hundreds or even thousands of dollars, with a record sale of one Pikachu card going for over $22 million earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Erielle Sudario

media:group.media:description: Alex Balykov is a trading card shop owner in Hughesdale, who started his business a few months ago.

item.title: Live: Young gun's breakout puts Saints in control against injury-hit Tigers
item.description: Hugo Garcia's wreaks havoc in the midfield as St Kilda inches towards an important win against Richmond. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva and Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: Hugo Garcia put together a stunning second quarter to put breathing room between St Kilda and Richmond.

item.title: Woman dead for weeks found in Hobart home
item.description: The body of a woman who has been dead for weeks has been found at a house in the Hobart suburb of New Town.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Snow

media:group.media:description: Tasmania Police says investigations are ongoing to determine the cause of a woman's death in New Town.

item.title: Qantas plane diverted after passenger bites flight attendant on way to US
item.description: Fellow passengers are believed to have assisted after a flight attendant was bitten on plane travelling between Melbourne and the US.

media:group.media:description: The passenger involved has been barred from travelling with Qantas in future.

item.title: Love story: How a punk grandma and an Aussie rocker found harmony
item.description: Singer/guitarist Mick Medew and self-proclaimed punk grandma Ursula Collie were in their mid-50s when they met and bonded over a mutual love of live music. A decade on, they're musical and life partners.

media:group.media:description: Mick Medew had accepted he was going to remain single before he met Ursula Collie.

item.title: Teenage pedestrian dies after being hit in Perth car park
item.description: A 19-year-old driver is being questioned by police after a teenager is struck by a car in a Perth shopping centre car park and dies in hospital.

media:group.media:description: Police remained at the car park in Bentley on Sunday, examining the scene.

item.title: Cakes and connection as migrant women get skills in kitchen and beyond
item.description: Beyond the Kitchen, a course run by Multicultural Services Centre of WA, gives women from migrant, refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds a safe space to develop friendships and hospitality business skills.
item.dc:creator: Mya Kordic and Lucy Martin

media:group.media:description: The women refine their cooking skills, while getting the chance to share it with others through opportunities like catering.

item.title: Labor claims victory in Queensland's Stafford by-election
item.description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond claims victory over the LNP's Fiona Hammond in a tight race in the north Brisbane electorate of Stafford.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Richards

media:group.media:description: Queensland Labor candidate Luke Richmond will be the next member for Stafford after claiming victory in the by-election.

item.title: Ebola kills 87 people as cases continue to rise in Congo
item.description: At least 87 deaths have been reported in Congo's new Ebola disease outbreak, as health authorities warn the strain "has no vaccine, no specific treatment".

media:group.media:description: Health officials are screening people in Kampala, Uganda, amid concerns the disease could rapidly spread.

item.title: Cameron Smith springs to life at golf major after rough week
item.description: Cameron Smith makes his move in the third round of the PGA Championship as he tries to end his major drought in the same week he cut ties with long-time coach Grant Field.

media:group.media:description: Cameron Smith ended his drought after missing the cut at six straight majors.

item.title: Police reopen cold case into armed robbery at Melbourne shopping centre
item.description: Police are appealing for help to find the man believed to be behind a 1994 armed robbery at a Chadstone cinema that left three other men with gunshot wounds.

media:group.media:description: Police are appealing for help to find the man believed to be behind the 1994 armed robbery.

item.title: Treasurer stands by CGT changes affecting shares and crypto
item.description: Jim Chalmers says it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes, and argued they can still access negative gearing by buying up new homes.
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers argues it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes.

item.title: Tourism operators priced out of own industry conference
item.description: Struggling businesses in Carnarvon, reliant on tourism, say they want more support to be involved with the 2026 WA Tourism Conference that the region is hosting.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Hagan

media:group.media:description: Cyclone Narelle and high fuel costs have negatively impacted tourism in Carnarvon.

item.title: Reef restoration boosts Swan River's health
item.description: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Davis and Emma Wynne

media:group.media:description: Brett Dal Pozzo says shellfish reefs are important for the river's health.

item.title: WA's first pair of fainting goats settle into new home
item.description: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.
item.dc:creator: Neve Brissenden and Madigan Landry

item.title: Police charge Qld man with murder after alleged assault
item.description: The 33-year-old Bundaberg North man was refused police bail after being charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hines

media:group.media:description: Emergency services were called to the Gooburrum property about 8pm on Friday.

item.title: From Euro-pop to Euro-slop, the key moments from Eurovision
item.description: Slept through Eurovision this year? Here's what you missed from Europe's favourite song contest, with boycotts, ballads and a Bulgarian song called Bangaranga making headlines.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Howard

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem's staging reflected her song, coming to a crescendo in the meeting of the Moon and the Sun.

item.title: Pro-Palestine march comes face-to-face with anti-immigration protest in London
item.description: Tens of thousands of people have marched through central London in two separate protests: one against immigration and another in support of Palestinians.

media:group.media:description: Demonstrators carry Palestinian flags and placards at a march to mark Nakba Day, commemorating Palestinians' loss of land in 1948.

item.title: Sydney lawyer ditches mortgage 'stress' for van life
item.description: Phoebe Macdougall says she hated living with a mortgage, so she is trying van life instead.
item.dc:creator: Nakita Jager

media:group.media:description: Phoebe Macdougall enjoys life on the road, and visits a number of campsites every year.

item.title: 'Not even one beer' for Man City after FA Cup triumph over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City's FA Cup celebrations after a 1-0 win over Chelsea will be low key, as the team refocuses on its tight Premier League title race with Arsenal.

media:group.media:description: Matheus Nunes may not have got Guardiola's memo.
